name: Río Nora River Trail
name_original: Senda Fluvial del río Nora — circular desde La Pola Siero
description: Jog this moderate 7.8-mile (12.5 km) route along the Río Nora River, enjoying scenic views, meadows, and the historic Recuna Medieval Bridge
region_intro: Jogging around Siero offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by green valleys and mountains, providing varied terrain for different fitness levels. The region features well-maintained trails, including popular riverside paths along the Río Nora. This varied topography ensures a range of options from gentle routes to more demanding mountain ascents. Siero's strategic location in Asturias provides access to broader natural attractions and scenic views. Best jogging routes around Siero The most popular jogging route is Río Nora River Trail, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 19 minutes to complete, offering scenic views and lush forests along the river. Another top favourite among local runners is Peña Careses Circular Trail, a moderate 4.7 miles (7.5 km) path, featuring varied Asturian scenery. Local runners also love the Old Bridge – San Miguel de Lillo loop from Lugones, a 14.2 miles (22.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 8 minutes. Jogging around Siero is defined by green valleys, riverside paths, and mountainous terrain, with a network of routes offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Siero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 900 runners have used komoot to explore Siero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes in Siero?
A: Siero offers a diverse range of terrain for runners. You'll find routes characterized by &lt;strong&gt;green valleys&lt;/strong&gt;, scenic &lt;strong&gt;riverside paths&lt;/strong&gt; along the Río Nora, and more challenging &lt;strong&gt;mountainous terrain&lt;/strong&gt;. This variety ensures options for different fitness levels, from gentle jogs to demanding ascents.

Q: Are there many running routes available in Siero?
A: Yes, Siero boasts a significant number of options for runners. There are nearly 60 dedicated running routes in the area, catering to various preferences and skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy running routes in Siero suitable for beginners?
A: Absolutely. Siero offers several easy running routes, with 7 trails specifically categorized as easy. These are perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed jog through the scenic Asturian landscape.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Siero?
A: The running trails in Siero are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average rating of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Runners frequently praise the varied terrain, from tranquil riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents, and the beautiful Asturian scenery.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Siero?
A: Yes, Siero features several circular running routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056085765/pena-careses-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Careses Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.5 km path that showcases varied Asturian scenery, including the historic San Pedrín Cave.

Q: Can I find running routes with significant elevation gain for a more challenging workout?
A: Certainly. For runners seeking a challenge, Siero offers several routes with substantial elevation gain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31883103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Bridge – San Miguel de Lillo loop from Lugones&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.8 km trail with over 950 meters of ascent, leading through mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any natural features or landmarks to see along the running paths in Siero?
A: Yes, many running paths in Siero offer scenic views and pass by interesting natural features and landmarks. You might encounter lush forests, historical bridges along the Río Nora, and even access points to areas like Monte Naranco, which provides spectacular panoramic views. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5570790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuensanta Natural Mineral Spring&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5152317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covarones of the Tuernes River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the running trails in Siero?
A: Yes, several routes offer excellent viewpoints. Trails leading towards areas like Monte Naranco, accessible from Siero, provide spectacular panoramic views of the surrounding areas, including Oviedo and Gijón. You can also find summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Praeres – Sierra de Peñamayor&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/337916" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gavío or Cimero&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Siero?
A: Siero's diverse natural landscapes make it appealing for running throughout much of the year. The region's green valleys and riverside paths are particularly pleasant during spring and autumn when temperatures are mild. Summer offers longer daylight hours, while winter conditions can vary, with mountainous areas potentially requiring more preparation.

Q: Are there any running routes that are particularly scenic or popular?
A: One of the most popular and scenic running routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056065167/rio-nora-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Nora River Trail&lt;/a&gt;. This 12.5 km trail offers beautiful scenic views and lush forests along the river, making it a favorite among local runners.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical sites or monuments?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical elements. For instance, sections of the Río Nora Fluvial Path pass by the historic Recuna Medieval Bridge. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40339558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Bridge – Christ of Naranco loop from Lugones&lt;/a&gt; leads towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/210761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacred Heart of Jesus Monument (Monte Naranco)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long are the running trails in Siero typically?
A: The running trails in Siero vary significantly in length to suit different preferences. You can find shorter routes, such as the 7.5 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056085765/pena-careses-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Careses Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, to much longer and more challenging options like the 22.8 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31883103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Bridge – San Miguel de Lillo loop from Lugones&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Pola de Siero is known for being an important industrial area, with a long tradition in the production of cider and other food products. In addition, it has numerous tourist attractions, such as the church of San Pedro, the Valdespino palace, the Mendoza palace, and the covered plaza, which is the center of the city's social and commercial life. La Pola Siero is also famous for its fiestas and festivals, including the Asturias Trade Fair, which is held in August, and the Cider Festival, which takes place in October.

The medieval bridge of Recuna is one of the medieval jewels of the Siero council. It gave access to the malataria, an important hospital institution that, in addition to lepers, was able to care for other patients and walkers. In addition, it is located on the route of one of the six main royal roads: the Camino Real from Villaviciosa to Pola de Siero within the Camino de Santiago.

The Nora River Path is a tourist and recreational route that follows the course of the Nora River. It is a well marked and conditioned path that offers panoramic views of the river, the meadows and the mountains in the background. Along the path, you can find numerous places of interest and there are also recreational areas with tables, benches and picnic areas to rest and enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Río Nora River Trail for jogging?
faqs.answer: While komoot classifies this specific 12.5 km route as moderate due to its distance and elevation gain of around 100 meters, the trail itself is generally considered easy. Its predominantly flat and paved surfaces make it accessible for most fitness levels, including beginners. However, the full circular route requires good physical condition for jogging.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features mostly paved or asphalted paths, making it very suitable for jogging. You might encounter some sections with dirt paths. The overall flatness of the route provides an easy and comfortable surface.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Río Nora River Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is easily accessible from Pola de Siero Town Center, which is a good starting point for this circular route. You can find parking options within the town to begin your jog.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: As you jog, you'll enjoy panoramic views of the river, lush meadows, and distant mountains. Keep an eye out for grazing horses, cows, and sheep. The route also passes through forests of oaks and other trees. Notable points include the historic Recuna Medieval Bridge and sections of the scenic Río Nora Riverside Trail. You might also spot old mills and industrial chimneys, reflecting the region's heritage.

faqs.question: Is the Río Nora River Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas or near livestock in meadows, to ensure the safety and comfort of all users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer is also good, but it's advisable to go early in the morning or late afternoon to avoid the midday heat. Winter jogging is possible, but be mindful of potentially cooler temperatures and any wet conditions on the paths.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to access the Río Nora River Trail. It is a public recreational path open to everyone.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with strollers?
faqs.answer: Many parts of the Río Nora River Trail, particularly the paved and flat sections, are suitable for pushing baby strollers. This makes it a great option for families looking for an accessible outdoor activity.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, starting and ending in Pola de Siero Town Center means you'll have access to various cafes, restaurants, and other amenities before or after your jog. There are also recreational areas with benches and picnic spots along the route.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates significant portions of the Senda fluvial del Río Nora (ciclista) and Senda fluvial del Río Nora (peatonal). It also briefly touches on sections of the Ruta Minera de Jovellanos and Camin de Careses, offering opportunities to explore broader landscapes if you wish to extend your adventure.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this route?
faqs.answer: This specific 12.5 km circular route is estimated to take approximately 1 hour and 20 minutes to complete at a jogging pace. Your actual duration may vary based on your speed and any stops you make to enjoy the scenery.
